Global (All).summary: Global economic instability is a major concern as trade tensions escalate between the US and China, with experts warning of a potential recession.
Rising nationalism and protectionism in major economies have led to increased tensions between countries, with a risk of escalating diplomatic conflicts.
The COVID-19 pandemic continues to pose a threat to global health, with ongoing outbreaks and vaccine distribution challenges in certain regions.

United States.title: Heat Waves in California Increase to 110+ Degrees
United States.summary: Mass power outages were reported as the extreme heat waves gripped the region.

United States.title: US Gun Sales Reach Record Highs Amid Increased Tensions
United States.summary: Following recent mass shootings and civil unrest, gun sales in the US have skyrocketed.

United States.title: Biden administration announces $800 million in new military aid to Ukraine
United States.summary: The fresh financial commitment is intended to help Ukraine resist Russian aggression.

South Korea.title: South Korea holds live-fire drill near border with North Korea amid rising tensions
South Korea.summary: South Korea holds a live-fire drill near its heavily fortified border with North Korea, amid heightened tensions following Pyongyang's recent missile tests.

South Korea.title: South Korea to deploy more F-35 fighter jets near North Korean border to deter threats
South Korea.summary: South Korea plans to deploy more F-35 fighter jets near its border with North Korea to deter potential threats from the North, the military said on Thursday.

South Korea.title: South Korean president calls on US to reconsider withdrawal of THAAD missile defense system
South Korea.summary: South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol has called for the US to rethink its plan to withdraw the Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) missile defense system from the country, citing North Korea's missile threats.
